title: Tyria salapa
taxonomy: Tyria salapa is classified within the family Thaumetopoeidae, which includes various species of moths. These moths are known as processionary caterpillars due to their unique behavior of moving in lines. Within the family Thaumetopoeidae, Tyria salapa is classified in the genus Tyria. The genus Tyria consists of several species of moths, primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Tyria salapa is further defined by its unique characteristics and biological features.

The health benefits of Tyrian purple include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The bioactive constituents of Tyrian purple include indole alkaloids, carotenoids, and other pigments. The most common herbal preparations made with Tyrian purple include tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, although its use is largely limited to cosmetic and textile applications due to the rarity and high cost of the dye.
